Scenario.title: Operation Ghost: Targeted Cyber Attack on Energy Sector

overview.description: Advanced persistent threat actors exploit multiple vulnerabilities to infiltrate and disrupt energy grid operations through a sophisticated multi-stage attack campaign.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of critical infrastructure for geopolitical purposes

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Internet-Facing SCADA Assets", "Employee Email", "SCADA Software Configuration", "Server Logs", "Shared Database", "Physical Grid Components"]
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: ["Man-in-The-Middle", "Social Engineering", "Application Compromise", "Inadequate Encryption", "Vulnerability Exploit", "Improper Configuration"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 163 Name: Spear Phishing Description: An adversary targets a specific user or group with a Phishing attack tailored to a category of users in order to have maximum relevance and deceptive capability.
stage.description: Attackers send spearphishing emails to energy sector employees, leveraging social engineering techniques to trick recipients into clicking on malicious links. These emails appear to come from trusted sources, such as internal departments or partners, making them difficult to detect.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 445 Name: Malicious Logic Insertion into Product Software via Configuration Management Manipulation Description: An adversary exploits a configuration management system to insert malicious logic into software updates or deployments.
stage.description: After gaining initial access through the phishing email, attackers deploy custom malware targeting exposed SCADA assets. This malware is designed to exploit known vulnerabilities in the SCADA software, enabling unauthorized access to critical control systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 552 Name: Install Rootkit Description: An adversary installs a rootkit to establish persistence and maintain control over the infected system.
stage.description: Attackers install a backdoor on the SCADA software configuration, ensuring they can re-establish access even after initial detection or removal attempts. This backdoor allows for continuous manipulation of the system.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 470 Name: Expanding Control over the Operating System from the Database Description: Attackers exploit database access to gain elevated privileges on the underlying operating system.
stage.description: By compromising database permissions, attackers escalate their privileges within the energy network, gaining access to sensitive systems and data that were previously restricted.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 93 Name: Log Injection-Tampering-Forging Description: Attackers manipulate log files to deceive audits and hide malicious activities.
stage.description: Attackers intercept and alter server logs to obscure their activities, preventing detection by security teams and creating a false trail of system integrity.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 440 Name: Hardware Integrity Attack Description: Attackers compromise hardware components to disrupt normal operations.
stage.description: Through application-level compromises, attackers trigger a shutdown of critical physical grid components, leading to widespread power outages and operational disruption.

Scenario.summary: Operation Ghost represents a highly coordinated and sophisticated cyber attack campaign targeting the energy sector. By combining social engineering, vulnerability exploitation, and advanced persistence techniques, the attackers successfully infiltrated and disrupted critical infrastructure, highlighting the growing threat of cyberattacks on essential services.
